fix(routing): add minimax to TOOL_CAPABLE_PROVIDERS + no azure escalation on OVH

Two bugs causing azure-openai calls on OVH even after routing fix:

1. TOOL_CAPABLE_PROVIDERS did not include 'minimax' - when best_provider=minimax
   and tools are present, it fell back to TOOL_PROVIDER=azure-openai.
   MiniMax M2.5 supports native function calling, so add it to the set.

2. High rejection rate escalation (>40%) always fell back to azure-openai,
   even on OVH where azure is unreachable. Gate it behind AZURE_DEPLOY.

fix: always queue fresh builds, never retry individual jobs

In-place build retries (PATCH ?retry=true) re-run individual jobs
which hit idempotency errors: artifact directories already existing,
partial state from previous runs, etc. The only reliable approach is
to queue a fresh top-level build.

RetryBuild now unconditionally calls queueFreshBuild. The artifact
detection and in-place retry codepath is removed entirely.

Consolidated 4 retry tests into 1 clean test that verifies fresh
build queuing.

Add learnings: spec ID workflow and full test suite requirement (#340)

Two learnings from PR review of ICM 756883304 hotfix:

1. Adding New Spec IDs Workflow: Always check the highest existing
   ID in requirements.md first, add there, then .c, then _ut.c.
   Run repo_validation after. Prevents ID conflicts and text mismatches.

2. Always Run Full Test Suite: When source changes affect a test
   project, run the entire suite, not just new/modified tests.
   Other tests may exercise different paths through the same code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use filesystem timestamps for actively running sessions

ListPersistedSessions was overriding the filesystem ModTime with
the DB's updated_at timestamp, which lags behind for sessions
actively running from the CLI. The DB only flushes updated_at at
turn boundaries, so a session mid-turn for an hour would appear
stale.

Now uses whichever timestamp is more recent (DB or filesystem),
and the hash function uses LastModified (which reflects filesystem
freshness) so the watcher detects activity from external CLI
sessions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Outputs: Markdown report, JSON, GitHub issues, Slack notifications

commit.message:
Replace pytz with stdlib zoneinfo across timezone-related code and docs (#41017)

`pytz` is a third-party dependency; Python 3.9+ ships `zoneinfo` in the
standard library as its replacement. This PR removes all `pytz`
references in favour of `zoneinfo`.

## Changes

- **`FacilityInfoTest.py`** — `test_timezones`: swap
`pytz.timezone(name)` → `ZoneInfo(name)` and `str(tz)` → `tz.key` for
the equality assertion
- **`FacilitiesFile.rst`** — update `timezone` attribute docs: link to
`zoneinfo` stdlib docs, reference `zoneinfo.available_timezones()`
instead of `pytz.all_timezones`
- **`FacilityInfo.h`** — update two comments (method doc + member
variable) from "compatible with pytz" → "compatible with zoneinfo"
- **`DateAndTime.rst`** — replace pytz code example and prose with
zoneinfo equivalent

```python
# Before
import pytz
tz = pytz.timezone(ConfigService.getFacility().timezone())
times = numpy.datetime_as_string(times, timezone=tz)

# After
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
tz = ZoneInfo(ConfigService.getFacility().timezone())
times = numpy.datetime_as_string(times, timezone=tz)
```

There is no associated issue, but this is related to
[EWM14929](https://ornlrse.clm.ibmcloud.com/ccm/web/projects/Neutron%20Data%20Project%20(Change%20Management)#action=com.ibm.team.workitem.viewWorkItem&id=14929)

### To test:

The examples should continue to work as-is.

commit.message:
Convert priority 3 MethodDescCallSite calls to UnmanagedCallersOnly (#124854)

First set of priority 3 conversions from #123864 — replace
`MethodDescCallSite`/`CallDescrWorker` calls with `UnmanagedCallersOnly`
reverse P/Invoke calls.

## Converted call sites

| File | Call site | Method |
|------|-----------|--------|
| `appdomain.cpp` | `AppDomain::RaiseExitProcessEvent` |
`ON_PROCESS_EXIT` |
| `appdomain.cpp` | `AppDomain::OnUnhandledException` |
`ON_UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION` |
| `assembly.cpp` | `RunManagedStartup` | `MANAGED_STARTUP` |
| `assembly.cpp` | `RunMainInternal` (TARGET_BROWSER) |
`HANDLE_ASYNC_ENTRYPOINT` |
| `invokeutil.cpp` | `InvokeUtil::CreateClassLoadExcept` |
`ReflectionTypeLoadException` ctor |
| `invokeutil.cpp` | `InvokeUtil::CreateTargetExcept` |
`TargetInvocationException` ctor |

## Remaining priority 3 items (follow ups)

- Main entry point in `RunMainInternal` (dynamic MethodDesc)
- `CustomAttribute_CreateCustomAttributeInstance` (dynamic ctor, already
a QCall)
- `CorHost2::ExecuteAssembly` (dynamic method lookup)
- `FuncEvalWrapper`/`DoNormalFuncEval` (debugger func-eval)

## Pattern

Follows the same conversion pattern as #124834 (priority 2). Each
conversion adds an `[UnmanagedCallersOnly]` managed wrapper with an
`Exception*` out-parameter and replaces the native `MethodDescCallSite`
+ `ARG_SLOT` with
`UnmanagedCallersOnlyCaller::InvokeThrowing`.

commit.message:
feat: MetadataCompiler — dense struct-of-arrays runtime tables (#1059)

Add MetadataCompiler that converts DataEntityMetadata/DataFieldMetadata into
cache-friendly ordinal-indexed runtime tables at startup:

- EntityTable: struct-of-arrays indexed by contiguous EntityId, sorted by slug
  with binary-search slug→EntityId resolver
- FieldTable: struct-of-arrays indexed by contiguous FieldId, entity-grouped
  with packed FieldFlags and WireFieldType mapping
- RouteTable: flat dispatch array indexed by (entityId << 4) | verbOrdinal
  for O(1) handler resolution
- RuntimeSnapshot: immutable container with atomic Interlocked.Exchange swap
- MetadataCompiler.Compile/CompileAndSwap: assigns contiguous IDs, builds all
  tables, returns frozen snapshot
- Wired into startup (after RuntimeEntityRegistry.Build) and gallery rebuild
- 11 unit tests covering empty/single/multi entity compilation, slug lookup,
  flag packing, wire-type mapping, route dispatch, and atomic swap

Closes #1056

commit.message:
chore(core-xml): bump fast-xml-parser minimum to ^5.3.7 to fix critical CVEs (#37489) `@azure/core-xml` declared `fast-xml-parser: ^5.0.7`, allowing versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 affected by two critical CVEs to satisfy the dependency range. ### Changes - **`sdk/core/core-xml/package.json`**: Raise minimum `fast-xml-parser` from `^5.0.7` → `^5.3.7` - **`sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md`**: Document security fix in unreleased `1.5.1` section ### Packages impacted by this PR `@azure/core-xml` ### Issues associated with this PR - [GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj) — DoS via unbounded entity expansion in DOCTYPE - [GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2) — Entity encoding bypass via regex injection in DOCTYPE entity names ### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR `fast-xml-parser` versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 have two critical vulnerabilities. The floating `^5.0.7` range allows any vulnerable 5.x version to be resolved. Raising the floor to `^5.3.7` ensures only patched versions are used. ### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen? Only option: update the minimum version constraint. No API changes required; `fast-xml-parser` 5.3.7 is backwards-compatible. ### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_ No. This is a dependency version floor bump with no behavioral changes — existing tests provide sufficient coverage. ### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_ Prior art: [1.3.4 bump of fast-xml-parser to `^4.2.4`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md#134-2023-06-20) for a prior security issue. ### Checklists - [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description. - [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here.)_ - [x] Added a changelog (if necessary).

We can update the minimum here, but in the meantime, you should be able to resolve this issue immediately by doing a dependency upgrade (`npm update`, etc.), which will allow you to pull in a patched version of `fast-xml-parser`. > > Let us know if you have any questions. > >

commit.message:
fix: accept Azure DevOps Git URLs without .git suffix in plugin marketplace (#299576)

* Initial plan

* fix: accept Azure DevOps Git URLs without .git suffix in plugin marketplace

- Remove the `.git` suffix requirement from `normalizeGitRepoPath`; the function
  now accepts any URL path that has at least two segments.
- Update `parseUriMarketplaceReference` to handle paths with and without `.git`:
  - `cacheSegments` are built without the suffix in both cases.
  - `canonicalId` is always normalized to include `.git` so that the same repo
    specified with and without the suffix deduplicates correctly.
- Add a JSDoc comment on `normalizeGitRepoPath` explaining the new semantics.
- Update the test that expected HTTPS/SSH URLs without `.git` to be rejected;
  these are now accepted. SCP-style (`git@host:path`) still requires `.git`.
- Add tests for Azure DevOps-style URLs and cross-suffix deduplication.
